plan.title: Deposit wired git hooks with mode 100755 so they fire on Unix consumers

narratives.Description: The git hooks wired by the installer are deposited non-executable (mode 100644), so on Linux and macOS git silently skips them and the branch-protection and destructive-push gates never fire for Unix consumers. This story deposits the hooks with mode 100755 and asserts POSIX executability so the gates actually run after install.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
Update the cmd/deft-install/githooks.go and setup.go modules so the deposited .githooks files are written and git-index-recorded with mode 100755, preserving the executable bit through the git-free file swap.
Extend the scripts/verify_hooks_installed.py module and its test to assert the resolved hooks are executable on POSIX rather than merely present.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft consumer on Linux or macOS, I want the wired git hooks to be executable, so that the branch-protection and destructive-push gates actually run on commit.

items.title: Given a fresh install on Linux or macOS, when a commit targets the default branch, then the executable pre-commit hook fires and blocks the commit.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a fresh install on Linux or macOS, when a commit targets the default branch, then the executable pre-commit hook fires and blocks the commit.

items.title: When the tracked hook deposit is inspected, then .githooks/pre-commit and .githooks/pre-push record mode 100755 and survive a subsequent --upgrade.

narrative.Acceptance: When the tracked hook deposit is inspected, then .githooks/pre-commit and .githooks/pre-push record mode 100755 and survive a subsequent --upgrade.

items.title: A regression test validates POSIX executability of the wired hooks and fails when the executable bit is missing.

narrative.Acceptance: A regression test validates POSIX executability of the wired hooks and fails when the executable bit is missing.

expected_outputs: regression test for the executable bit

references.title: Issue #1477: Wired git hooks ship non-executable (mode 100644) -- silently inert on Unix consumers (follow-up to #1463)
